title: Applying machine learning to detector data
abstract: The present invention uses machine learning algorithms to analyse data obtained from detectors in fire protection systems, e.g. fire detectors, smoke detectors and/or carbon monoxide detectors. Methods of the disclosure predict a time at which maintenance of 5 one or more of the detectors is required, based on the analysis. This time may be a present time (i.e. maintenance of a detector is required immediately) or a future time (i.e. maintenance of a detector is going to be required). These methods ensure that the fire detection system receives timely maintenance, thereby maximizing system uptime and reliability. 0
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A method comprising : receiving data generated by one or more detectors; applying one or more machine learning algorithms to the data to identify one or more trends in the data, wherein applying the one or more machine learning algorithms to the data comprises: grouping the data into one or more clusters, and analysing the data in at least one of the one or more clusters to determine a rate of change of a distance of a data point from a centroid of the cluster, the data point being associated with a first detector of the one or more detectors; comparing the rate of change of the distance of the data point from the centroid of the cluster against a predetermined threshold; and determining, based on the rate of change of the distance of the data point from the centroid of the cluster exceeding the predetermined threshold, that maintenance of the first detector is required. 2. A method according to claim 1, further comprising outputting a notification indicating that maintenance of the first detector is currently required. 3. A method according to any of claim 1 or 2, wherein the machine learning algorithms comprise one or more of: a support vector machine, a K-nearest neighbours algorithm and an autoencoder. 4. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the data generated by the detectors comprise multidimensional data. 5. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the data generated by the detectors comprise one or more of: obscuration data, temperature data, carbon monoxide data and time data. 6. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the one or more detectors comprise a plurality of detectors located at the same site, optionally wherein the plurality of detectors include at least one first detector located in a first zone at the site and at least one second detector located in a second zone at the site. 7. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the one or more detectors comprise a plurality of detectors of the same type, or a plurality of detectors of different types. 8. A method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the one or more detectors comprise one or more of: smoke detectors; heat detectors; flame detectors and carbon monoxide detectors. 9. A system comprising : one or more processors; and a memory storing instructions that, when executed by the one or more processors, cause the one or more processors to: receive data generated by one or more detectors; apply one or more machine learning algorithms to the data to identify one or more trends in the data, wherein applying the one or more machine learning algorithms to the data comprises: grouping the data into one or more clusters, and analysing the data in at least one of the one or more clusters to determine a rate of change of a distance of a data point from a centroid of the cluster, the data point being associated with a first detector of the one or more detectors; compare the rate of change of the distance of the data point from the centroid of the cluster against a predetermined threshold; and determine, based on the rate of change of the distance of the data point from the centroid of the cluster exceeding the predetermined threshold, that maintenance of the first detector is required. 10. A computer-readable medium comprising instructions that, when executed by one or more processors, cause the one or more processors to: receive data generated by one or more detectors; apply one or more machine learning algorithms to the data to identify one or more trends in the data, wherein applying the one or more machine learning algorithms to the data comprises: grouping the data into one or more clusters, and analysing the data in at least one of the one or more clusters to determine a rate of change of a distance of a data point from a centroid of the cluster, the data point being associated with a first detector of the one or more detectors; compare the rate of change of the distance of the data point from the centroid of the cluster against a predetermined threshold; and 

 determine, based on the rate of change of the distance of the data point from the centroid of the cluster exceeding the predetermined threshold, that maintenance of the first detector is required.
